degraded.detail: The diff touches only shipwright-grade (resolve_target.py, grade.py, gh_bridge.py) + its tests + docs; grep confirmed no consumers of the changed symbols (input_kind, is_github_com_remote) outside the grade plugin. F0 ran the shipwright-grade suite (373 passed) + ruff@0.15.15 + leak-guard f0 ALLOW. Compliance/shared/integration are unchanged and run in CI on the PR.

The network-on default was proven with a live call: grading https://github.com/octocat/Hello-World WITHOUT --allow-network returned network_enabled=True (PR-association enrichment). gh authed as svroch. This is the feature under test (network-on for public github.com URLs); only public-repo identity + slug were sent, to github.com, which the clone already contacted.

surface_verification.justification: The change is to the shipwright-grade CLI wrapper (grade.py network-policy wiring) + the input seam (resolve_target.open_target tagging) + a gh_bridge host predicate. No web/UI/API/server surface. The CLI grader IS the executable surface and was driven END-TO-END: (1) a public github.com URL (octocat/Hello-World) WITHOUT --allow-network -> network_enabled=True (default flip works); (2) a local path to this monorepo (public remote) -> network_enabled=False (privacy-first intact, graded A authoritative); (3) a URL clone with no github.com remote -> local grade B, network off (honest fallback, never F); (4) a GitHub Enterprise host is excluded from the default (unit-tested -- its slug must not leak to github.com). Verification = F0 pytest (373) + ruff + leak-guard f0 ALLOW + 3 real-CLI grades.

behaviors.behavior: AC1 -- a cloned remote self-describes input_kind='url'
AC2 -- a local path stays input_kind='local_path'
AC1 -- a public github.com URL defaults allow_network=True
PRIVACY -- a GitHub Enterprise / non-github.com URL is NOT defaulted network-on (its slug must not leak to github.com)
is_github_com_remote matches ONLY literal github.com (https/ssh/scp), rejects GHE/spoofed/empty/local-path
AC2 -- a local path defaults allow_network=False (privacy-first, even with a public remote)
AC3 -- --allow-network-private stays opt-in (allow_private False by default)
behaviors.evidence: the policy spy asserts allow_private is False on the URL path

behaviors.behavior: AC4 -- a URL clone without a github.com remote -> honest local grade, never F (hermetic, no gh call)
Regression -- the existing URL clone-and-grade still grades B and is now hermetic
